review_summary: The transcript is sufficient for concept questions on classifier-free guidance and why non-uniform noise schedules are often preferred in diffusion training.

question_bank.question: Why does classifier-free guidance randomly drop the condition during training?
choices: So one model learns both unconditional and conditional denoising directions
So the model never sees the conditioning signal at all
So sampling can be done without any noise variable
So the model can estimate an exact likelihood p(x)
question_bank.explanation: Dropping the condition teaches the model how to behave both with and without conditioning information. That makes it possible to combine the two predictions at sampling time to strengthen guidance.

question_bank.question: Why are non-uniform noise schedules often preferred over sampling t uniformly?
choices: Because uniform sampling spends equal effort on easy endpoints and harder middle-noise cases
Because uniform sampling makes the model ignore the data distribution entirely
Because uniform sampling is only valid for GANs, not diffusion models
Because non-uniform schedules remove the need for timestep conditioning
question_bank.explanation: Not all noise levels are equally difficult. Practical schedules often emphasize the parts of the denoising problem that are harder or more important for a given resolution and model setup.
